name: hebbian-weights
hypothesis: Updating relation weights based on entity co-activation across sessions will produce more semantically coherent communities than static co-occurrence weights
grade_notes: Correct semantic signal. yt_dj↔Liquidsoap, music-genome↔genre-curation-skill, Cortex↔power-search all emerge correctly. 160→145 communities, avg size +10%. Worth integrating but ETA=0.3 may be too conservative — experiment 002 should try higher learning rate or lower MIN_COACTIVATIONS.
eval_verdict: The Hebbian update rule is saturated at Δw=3.0 across all pairs regardless of co-activation magnitude, meaning the experiment produced a binary presence detector rather than a weighted associative memory — the core hypothesis is not proven.
